Row1.Pathway ID/Name: EGFR signaling pathway (KEGG hsa04012; Reactome R-HSA-177929)
Row1.Rationale: Necitumumab binds the extracellular domain of EGFR, blocking ligand binding, dimerization, and phosphorylation, thereby attenuating EGFR signaling outputs that drive proliferation and survival in EGFR‑overexpressing NSCLC, particularly squamous tumors.

Row2.Rationale: EGFR activates the MAPK cascade; necitumumab reduces this signaling, but activating alterations in KRAS/BRAF or downstream MAPK components maintain pathway activity independent of EGFR, limiting benefit from EGFR‑directed antibodies.

Row3.Rationale: EGFR signals through PI3K–AKT–mTOR to promote growth and survival; necitumumab decreases pathway activation, but PIK3CA mutation/amplification or PTEN loss uncouple PI3K–AKT from EGFR, sustaining survival despite receptor inhibition.

Row4.Rationale: EGFR and IL‑6R–JAK activate STAT3, which supports survival and early adaptive resistance; transient STAT3 activation after EGFR inhibition can blunt responses, and combined STAT3 blockade enhances EGFR‑targeted therapy in lung cancer models.

Row5.Rationale: EGFR‑driven and TNF‑mediated NF‑κB activation induces pro‑survival transcriptional programs; persistent NF‑κB activity under EGFR blockade provides an alternative survival route, contributing to intrinsic or acquired resistance to necitumumab.

Row6.Rationale: EGFR protein is overexpressed in a majority of NSCLCs and is particularly frequent in squamous histology; necitumumab’s clinical benefit in SQUIRE is confined to this EGFR‑expressing squamous population, supporting a dependence of drug sensitivity on elevated EGFR signaling.

Row7.Pathway ID/Name: EGFR‑TKI / anti‑EGFR antibody resistance network (KEGG hsa01521)
Row7.Rationale: Resistance mechanisms to EGFR‑directed agents include KRAS/NRAS/BRAF and PIK3CA mutations, PTEN loss, MET/HER2 amplification, ligand overexpression, and EMT; these alterations restore downstream signaling or bypass EGFR, and are expected to similarly constrain necitumumab efficacy.

Row8.Pathway ID/Name: MET / bypass RTK signaling (Reactome R-HSA-6806834; KEGG hsa04010 MET module)
Row8.Rationale: MET and other RTKs (HER2, AXL, FGFR1) can activate MAPK and PI3K–AKT independently of EGFR; amplification or overactivation of these receptors mediates escape from EGFR antibodies, sustaining growth despite necitumumab treatment.

Row9.Rationale: EMT reduces epithelial EGFR dependence and is cited as a resistance mechanism to EGFR mAbs; EMT‑associated AXL/FGFR1 upregulation and invasive phenotypes allow NSCLC cells to proliferate and migrate despite EGFR blockade.

Row10.Pathway ID/Name: ADCC / FcγR‑mediated effector pathway (MSigDB NK cell / FcγR signaling)
Row10.Rationale: Necitumumab is an IgG1 antibody capable of engaging Fcγ receptors on NK and myeloid cells to mediate ADCC and phagocytosis; effective FcγR engagement and robust innate effector function can augment direct EGFR blockade, enhancing tumor clearance.

Row11.Rationale: The HLA‑G–ILT2/ILT4 pathway suppresses NK‑cell ADCC and myeloid ADCP, key mechanisms for IgG1 antibodies; upregulation of ILT4+ myeloid and ILT2+ lymphoid cells in NSCLC can dampen necitumumab‑mediated effector functions, reducing clinical activity.

Row12.Rationale: EGFR signaling in renal tubules regulates magnesium reabsorption; necitumumab‑induced EGFR blockade causes hypomagnesemia and related electrolyte abnormalities, which can necessitate dose interruption or discontinuation, indirectly constraining dose intensity and therapeutic benefit.
